LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE SEP-06-2022 HEARING RE: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO PLAINTIFF ORLANDO MORALES, AFTER THE HEARING, THE COURT RECEIVES AN EMAIL FROM THE MOVING PARTY INFORMING THE COURT THAT THEIR MOTION WAS NOTICED FOR SEPTEMBER 26TH, NOT SEPTEMBER 6TH. UPON FILING THE MOTION, THE CIVIL CLERK'S OFFICE'S EFILING DIVISION ERRONEOUSLY SET A HEARING IN THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS FOR SEPTEMBER 6TH. AS SUCH, AND PER THE FILED NOTICE, THE COURT CALENDARS THIS MATTER FOR HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 26, 2022 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, AND CONFIRMS THIS FOR PARTIES BY REPLY EMAIL. (302/RBU) *****
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-26-2022 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-09-2022 Dismissal - No Appearance

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Ronald L. Zambrano, Esq., WEST COAST EMPLOYMENT LAWYERS, APLC, 213-927-3701, for Plaintiff Maricela Ponce, et al.
No Other appearances.

Hearing is held. Not ARGUED. Counsel, Ronald L Zambrano, informs the Court that Department 206 allows the hearing of this motion in Department 302. (current trial date is set for November 9, 2022). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ORLANDO MORALES MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is GRANTED.

Counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order using the Mandatory JCC Form for Motion for Withdrawal of Counsel.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-06-2022 Continued To Sep-26-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO PLAINTIFF ORLANDO MORALES.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar as untimely pursuant to CCP 1005.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***** After the hearing, the Court receives an email from the moving party informing the Court that their motion was noticed for September 26th, not September 6th. Upon filing the motion, the Civil Clerk's Office's efiling division erroneously set a hearing in the Register of Actions for September 6th. As such, and per the filed notice, the Court calendars this matter for hearing on September 26, 2022 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, and confirms this for parties by reply email. (302/RBU) *****
